title: Cairn corpus.lock (cairn-corpus-lock/1.0)
description: The pinned, ordered, curated selection of case bundles that assemble into one corpus. The engine owns this schema; an instance (corpus.lock) lives in a corpus repo (e.g. cairns/corpus). `cairn corpus assemble <corpus.lock>` resolves each entry, verifies its digest + structure + engine compatibility, and assembles CASES.json/INDEX.json in `cases` order. See CORPUS-SPEC.md.

corpus_lock_version.description: The cairn-corpus-lock contract version this file targets.

corpus.description: Corpus-level metadata + the default compatibility keys every entry inherits.

name.description: Stable identifier for this curated corpus (e.g. 'epistack-flf-refuse-to-combine'). A corpus TAG (name@vX.Y) is a reproducible artifact: exactly these entries assembling to one hash.

domain.description: Optional deployed identity of this corpus, in the 'domain is identity' model (e.g. 'corpus.a.epistack.dev'). A locator/owner, never a trust root -- the assembled hash and the per-entry digests remain authoritative for content. Reserved; the resolver lands with subdomain-delegation infra. See CORPUS-SPEC.md §8.

record_schema.description: The record-envelope schema line every entry's records conform to. Load-bearing: Trusty-URIs are hashes over the canonical form under this schema. Must match each entry's engine.pin `record_schema`.

canonicalization.description: The canonical byte-form the records are content-addressed and signed over. A different backend drifts every URI.

cases.description: The ordered selection. Array order IS assembly order and fixes the line order of the aggregate CASES.json/INDEX.json -- byte-identity depends on it. Case ids must be unique; JSON Schema cannot enforce cross-item uniqueness, so assembly rejects a duplicate case_id and names the collision (CORPUS-SPEC.md §2).

entry.description: One pinned case. Exactly one resolution mode: `repo`+`ref` (clone-and-verify at a pinned commit/tag), `path` (a local bundle in the working tree), or `domain` (the deployed-domain identity, reserved). `digest` is mandatory in every mode and is the trust root.

case_id.description: The bundle's case id -- its directory name and its (unique) key in CASES.json.

repo.description: The case repo to clone (e.g. 'cairns/covid-origins', a slug or clone URL). Present only in repo mode.

ref.description: The immutable git ref pinned for this entry -- a tag or full commit SHA (a branch name is NOT reproducible and is rejected by assembly). Required in repo mode; must be absent in local and domain modes.

path.description: A working-tree-relative path to the bundle directory. Present only in local mode (`repo`/`ref`/`domain` must be absent). Lets a corpus.lock pin the in-tree bundles so assembly is provable before any case repo is stood up.

domain.description: The case's deployed-domain identity (e.g. 'covid-origins.a.epistack.dev'), in the 'domain is identity' model. A locator/owner; the `digest` remains the trust root. RESERVED: the schema accepts it, but the domain resolver + hosting land with subdomain-delegation infra (CORPUS-SPEC.md §8). Present only in domain mode (`repo`/`ref`/`path` must be absent).

digest.description: The bundle content digest (cairn.cases.bundle_digest: sha256 over CASE.json/build.py/PROVENANCE.md/engine.pin + records/* + sources/*, in stable order). Assembly recomputes it after resolving the entry and fails loudly on any drift, so a repo/domain cannot silently change under a pin -- and any mirror satisfying the digest reproduces the bytes.

engine.description: The record-schema compatibility key pinned for this entry. Defaults to corpus.record_schema when absent; when present it must equal both corpus.record_schema and the case's own engine.pin `record_schema`. Assembly refuses on disagreement.
